title: 10 best herbal capsules for neuritis
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for neuritis are a natural remedy that helps alleviate symptoms associated with inflammation of the nerves, such as numbness, tingling, and pain. These capsules typically contain a blend of herbs like turmeric, ginger, and ginkgo biloba, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. For example, turmeric contains curcumin, which has been shown to reduce nerve damage and promote healing. Taking these herbal capsules can help improve daily life by reducing discomfort, improving mobility, and enhancing overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with neuritis because they contain a unique combination of active compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The capsules' extracts, particularly adiantum, have been traditionally used to soothe nerve pain and reduce inflammation in the nervous system. By targeting the root cause of neuritis, these capsules can help alleviate symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and weakness, promoting a faster recovery and reducing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Ginkgo biloba capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nervous system, which can contribute to neuritis.

Vasodilatory Effects: The extract from Maidenhair Tree capsules dilates blood vessels, improving blood circulation to the brain and nerve tissue, which may alleviate symptoms of neuritis such as numbness, tingling, and weakness.

Neuroprotective Compounds: Ginkgo biloba contains compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids that have been shown to protect neurons from damage and promote neuronal survival, potentially helping to mitigate the progression of neuritis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nerves, which can contribute to neuritis.
Bilobalide: This triterpene constituent has neuroprotective properties, helping to reduce neuronal damage and promote the health of nerve cells.
Quercetin: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high flavonoid and terpene content, which are believed to have neuroprotective effects.
Seeds: The seeds are also widely used as they contain ginkgolides, compounds that have been shown to improve blood flow to the brain and reduce inflammation associated with neuritis.
Buds: The buds are sometimes used as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which may contribute to the plant's neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves by spreading them evenly on a baking sheet in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Mix the ginkgo biloba powder with a small amount of cellulose powder to create a uniform blend using a ratio of 1:1.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the ginkgo biloba powder mixture using a spoon or a small measuring cup.
Label and store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort capsules helps with neuritis because they contain hyperforin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When taken orally in capsule form, hyperforin is absorbed into the bloodstream and can reach the affected nerves, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with conditions such as peripheral neuropathy and neuritis. This can help alleviate symptoms like pain, numbness, and tingling, promoting faster recovery and improved nerve function.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Hyperforin has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.
Hypotensive compounds: The hypotensive compounds present in St. John's Wort capsules can help to reduce blood pressure and alleviate compression on the nerves, thereby relieving symptoms of neuritis.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of St. John's Wort can help to protect the nerves from damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to the development of neuritis.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, alleviating symptoms of neuritis.
Naringenin: Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, protecting neurons from damage and promoting healing in cases of neuritis.
Quercetin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, reducing pain and inflammation associated with neuritis, while also promoting the repair of damaged nerve tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of hypericin and hyperforin, which are believed to have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Rich in hypericin and hyperforin, they are used to create capsules that help alleviate symptoms of neuritis due to their potential to reduce inflammation and promote nerve regeneration.
Seeds: They contain a high concentration of flavonoids, particularly quercetin, which may contribute to their neuroprotective effects and ability to reduce inflammation in neuritis cases.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom.
Grind the harvested material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 5 grams of the powder with 5 grams of gelatin in a small bowl to create the capsule filling.
Use a capsule machine to fill 100-200 capsules with the mixture and seal them tightly.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place for up to 12 months or use them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi capsules helps with neuritis because they contain an extract of Bacopa monnieri, a natural herb that has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to enhance cognitive function. The herb is known to improve memory, concentration, and mental clarity by promoting the growth of new neurons and strengthening existing neural connections. This can help alleviate symptoms of neuritis such as numbness, tingling, and weakness, allowing individuals to regain their normal neurological functioning.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Brahmi's antioxidant property helps protect nerve cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to neuritis and its symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory property: Brahmi's anti-inflammatory property reduces inflammation in the nerves, which can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.

Neuroprotective property: Brahmi's neuroprotective property helps preserve the integrity of nerve cells, promoting healthy nerve function and reducing the risk of nerve damage or degeneration associated with neuritis.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides are a group of saponin glycosides present in Bacopa monnieri, and they help with neuritis by promoting neuronal repair and reducing oxidative stress.
Bacopaside II: Bacopaside II is an alkaloid present in Bacopa monnieri, and it helps with neuritis by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing inflammation.
Bacoside A: Bacoside A is a saponin glycoside present in Bacopa monnieri, and it helps with neuritis by improving neuronal transmission and reducing neurodegeneration.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most used part due to their high bacoside content, which is responsible for the medicinal properties of Bacopa monnieri.
Roots: The roots are used due to their bacoside content and are often dried and processed to make capsules for neuritis.
Stems: The stems are another used part, although to a lesser extent, and contain bacosides that contribute to the plant's medicinal properties.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of dried bacopa monnieri root and 100 grams of gelatin powder on a digital scale.
Grind 500 grams of dried bacopa monnieri root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of gelatin powder and 50 grams of water in a heat-resistant bowl and heat it in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Combine 500 grams of ground bacopa monnieri powder and 200 grams of the gelatin mixture in a stainless steel bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the bacopa monnieri mixture and store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng capsules helps with neuritis because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and promote nerve health. The herb's active compounds, such as ginsenosides, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals that contribute to nerve damage. Additionally, ginseng has been found to improve blood flow to the nerves, which can help alleviate symptoms of neuritis such as numbness, tingling, and weakness. By addressing these underlying factors, ginseng capsules provide a natural and effective solution for managing neuritis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginseng's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in nerve tissue, which can alleviate symptoms of neuritis such as pain, numbness, and tingling.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginseng's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as ginsenosides, reduce inflammation in the nerves and surrounding tissues, which can help relieve discomfort and prevent further damage.

Nerve-protective and neuroregenerative properties: Ginseng has been shown to promote nerve regeneration and protect against nerve damage, which can help restore function and alleviate symptoms of neuritis.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: Ginsenosides, particularly ginsenoside Rg1, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate neuritis symptoms.
Ginsenoside Rb1: Ginsenoside Rb1 has neuroprotective effects, which help prevent oxidative stress and damage to nerve cells, thereby reducing neuritis symptoms.
Panaxynol: Panaxynol, a lignan, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and protect nerve cells from damage, contributing to the relief of neuritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, particularly ginsenosides.
Rhyzomes: They contain similar bioactive compounds as the roots and are sometimes used in combination to enhance efficacy.
Leaves: Although less commonly used than roots and rhyzomes, leaves are sometimes used due to their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh panax ginseng roots in late summer or early fall when they are mature.
Dry the roots in a cool dry place for 2-3 weeks to prevent spoilage and preserve potency.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with a suitable capsule filler material in a 1:2 ratio by weight to fill capsules.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine for precise control.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha capsules helps with neuritis because they contain a potent adaptogenic herb that reduces inflammation in the nervous system. Neuritis is characterized by inflammation of the nerves, which can cause debilitating pain and discomfort. The antioxidant properties of ashwagandha help to combat oxidative stress, thus reducing nerve damage and promoting healing. Additionally, ashwagandha's ability to calm the body's response to stress also helps to alleviate symptoms such as anxiety and fatigue that often accompany neuritis.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Ashwagandha helps reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate neuritis symptoms, by promoting relaxation and normalizing cortisol levels.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and pain associated with neuritis by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Neuroprotective effects: Ashwagandha contains antioxidants and other neuroprotective compounds that can help protect against nerve damage, promote nerve regeneration, and improve overall nervous system function.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These steroidal lactones help with neuritis by reducing inflammation and promoting nerve regeneration.
Withaferin A: This alkaloid has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help protect nerve cells from damage and promote recovery from neuritis.
Ashwagandhanso A: This alkaloid has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including withanolides, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties.
Seeds: They are sometimes used as they contain withanolides and other beneficial compounds, although their usage is less common compared to roots.
Leaves: They are occasionally used as they possess some of the same bioactive compounds found in the roots, although their effectiveness and usage are less documented.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of dried withania somnifera root powder to use in capsule preparation.
Mix 100 grams of the root powder with 200 grams of cellulose powder in a bowl.
Add 50 milliliters of water to the mixture and stir until the cellulose powder is well incorporated.
Fill a capsule machine with the herbal mixture and press the buttons to form capsules.
Label and store the 100 withania somnifera capsules in an airtight container for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with neuritis because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that targets the root cause of the condition. Curcumin's potent antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nerve tissue, alleviating symptoms such as pain, numbness, and tingling. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to enhance blood flow to the affected nerves, promoting healthy nerve function and regeneration. By addressing these underlying causes of neuritis, turmeric capsules can provide effective relief from this debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Turmeric's active compound curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, alleviating symptoms of neuritis.

Bioavailability enhancers: Herbal turmeric capsules often contain bioavailability enhancers like piperine or black pepper extract, which improve the absorption of curcuminoids into the bloodstream, allowing them to reach affected areas more effectively.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds: Turmeric's other compounds, such as gingerols and sesquiterpenes, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that complement curcumin's effects, providing additional benefits for nerve health and neuritis relief.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate neuritis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, which may help prevent or slow down the progression of neuritis by protecting nerve cells from damage and promoting their survival.

Volitin (Curcumenol): Volitin has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, thereby alleviating neuritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes are widely used due to their high curcumin content, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate neuritis symptoms.

Roots: Curcuma longa roots are another commonly used part, as they contain a high concentration of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and pain associated with neuritis.
Leaves: The leaves of Curcuma longa are used in some herbal remedies for neuritis, as they contain compounds that may help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nervous system.
remedy_recipe: Grind 10 grams of dried curcuma longa rhizomes into fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with 10 grams of vegetable cellulose in a stainless steel bowl for 5 minutes.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and set the machine to 500mg capsules for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for 30 minutes.
Label and package the capsules in airtight containers for later use within 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense capsules helps with neuritis because they contain boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that effectively reduce swelling and pain in the nerves. The capsules also possess analgesic and antispasmodic effects, providing relief from numbness, tingling, and muscle weakness often associated with neuritis. Additionally, frankincense has been shown to improve blood flow to the affected areas, promoting healing and regeneration of damaged nerve tissues. This natural supplement can provide significant relief for individuals suffering from neuritis-related discomfort and pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense capsules contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce swelling and pain in the nerves associated with neuritis.
Antioxidant: The capsules' high antioxidant content helps protect against oxidative stress and damage to nerve tissues, which can contribute to the development of neuritis.
Anti-nociceptive: Frankincense has been shown to have analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties, reducing pain perception and discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedy_constituents: Acylic triterpenoids: They help with neuritis by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, which in turn alleviates pain and discomfort associated with nerve inflammation.

Boswellic acids: They help with neuritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the nerves, and providing relief from pain and numbness.

Terpenoids (specifically, beta-boswellic acid): They help with neuritis by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the nerves, and providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with nerve inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Boswellia serrata are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Boswellia serrata are also used due to their high boswellic acid content, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with neuritis.

Barks: The barks of Boswellia serrata are used as a secondary option, as they contain lower levels of boswellic acids compared to the roots and rhyzomes, but still offer some anti-inflammatory benefits.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of boswellia serrata resin powder on a digital scale with an accuracy of 0.1 grams.
Combine 500 grams of the powder with 500 milliliters of glycerin in a stainless steel mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes with constant stirring.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a glass container and discard the solids.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 250 milligrams of the boswellia serrata extract and seal the capsules tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree capsules helps with neuritis because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling in the nerves. The herbal extract has also been shown to improve nerve function and promote healing by increasing blood flow to the affected area. Additionally, chaste tree's hormonal balancing effects can help to alleviate symptoms of neuritis caused by hormonal imbalances or fluctuations.
remedy_properties: Daucosterol: May reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with neuritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and promoting anti-inflammatory compounds.
Vitexin: Has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect against nerve damage and reduce symptoms of neuritis such as numbness, tingling, and weakness.
Flavonoids: May act as a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent by inhibiting the production of pain-causing chemicals and reducing swelling in the affected nerves.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help with neuritis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, which can contribute to nerve damage and discomfort.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as vitexin and orientin, in Vitex agnus castus capsules have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect against nerve damage and promote healing in cases of neuritis.

Acetyl-tyrosol: Acetyl-tyrosol, a phenolic compound found in Vitex agnus castus, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are considered to have a high concentration of bioactive compounds that may help alleviate neuritis symptoms.
Leaves: They are believed to contain flavonoids and other compounds that may contribute to their neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: They are traditionally used due to their potential to provide antioxidant and neuroprotective effects, which may aid in managing neuritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried vitex agnus castus flowers or leaves with a sharp knife in late summer.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Grind 1-2 grams of the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1/4 teaspoon of the powder with 1/4 teaspoon of a natural capsule filler like rice flour in a small bowl.
Fill 10-20 empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a small spoon and a capsule filling device.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with neuritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds present in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and pain associated with neuritis, a condition characterized by nerve damage and irritation. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, English lavender capsules also help alleviate symptoms of neuritis, including numbness, tingling, and burning sensations, providing relief for individuals suffering from this debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English Lavender capsules contain compounds like linalool, which help reduce inflammation in the nervous system, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.
Anxiolytic: The calming effects of English Lavender can help soothe anxiety and stress, which are common symptoms of neuritis, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of English Lavender capsules can help relieve spasms and cramping in the nerves, providing relief from numbness, tingling, and other unpleasant sensations associated with neuritis.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with neuritis, thereby alleviating symptoms such as pain and numbness.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has a sedative and calming effect, which helps to reduce anxiety and stress that can exacerbate neuritis symptoms, promoting relaxation and pain relief.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nervous system, alleviating symptoms of neuritis such as pain, numbness, and tingling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make capsules for neuritis due to their high content of antioxidants and essential oils that have anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make capsules for neuritis due to their high content of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have sedative and anti-inflammatory effects.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make capsules for neuritis due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at the peak of their potency and freshness.
Grind 15g of the harvested flowers into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix the ground powder with 5g of vegetable cellulose as a capsule filler.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the powder mixture using a capsule filler machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with neuritis because they contain Passiflora incarnata, a herb that has been traditionally used to soothe nervous tension and alleviate pain. The plant's active compounds, including flavonoids and glycosides, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort in the nerves, providing relief from symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and burning sensations. By calming the nervous system, maypop capsules can also help regulate nerve function and promote overall relaxation and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Maypop capsules contain flavonoids and saponins that have been shown to reduce inflammation, which is a common contributor to neuritis pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in maypop capsules help protect against oxidative stress and damage to nerve cells, which can contribute to the development of neuritis and other neurodegenerative conditions.
Pain-relieving properties: Maypop contains compounds that have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to relieve pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedy_constituents: Harmane: A phenolic alkaloid compound that has a sedative effect, which can help alleviate the pain and inflammation associated with neuritis by reducing nerve excitability and promoting relaxation.

Flavonoids: A class of compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, promoting recovery from neuritis.

Lignans: A type of phenolic compound that has a sedative and anti-inflammatory effect, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with neuritis, as well as promote relaxation and reduce muscle spasms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds with potential neuroprotective effects.
Stems: Utilized for their rich source of flavonoids, which may contribute to their neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves from a clean environment on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested material at 30-40°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture and ensure preservation.
Grind the dried passiflora incarnata into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-500mg of the powder and seal them securely with a capsule sealer.
Store the passiflora incarnata capsules in a cool dark place for up to 1 year to maintain potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with neuritis because they contain compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. The valerenic acid in these capsules has been shown to interact with GABA receptors, helping to regulate nerve impulses and ease nerve pain and numbness. This natural approach can provide relief from the discomfort and tingling associated with neuritis, allowing individuals to manage their symptoms and improve overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anxiolytic properties: Valerian root's sedative and anxiolytic properties can help to reduce nerve tension and inflammation associated with neuritis, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.

Pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian root has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate the pain and swelling associated with neuritis.

Nerve-calming and neuroprotective properties: Valerian root's ability to calm and protect nerves can help to reduce nerve damage and promote healing in cases of neuritis, potentially reducing the risk of long-term complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help alleviate nerve pain associated with neuritis.
Rhizomes: The rhizome of the valerian plant contains a higher concentration of sesquiterpenes than the roots, making it particularly effective in reducing inflammation and relieving nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots in autumn or spring when the plant is at its peak potency.
Clean and chop the harvested roots into small pieces and then dry them for at least 24 hours.
Transfer the dried root material to a coffee grinder or spice grinder and grind it into a fine powder using 10-15 minutes of high-speed grinding.
Sift the ground valeriana officinalis powder through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any lumps or large particles and ensure uniformity.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 400mg of the sifted valeriana officinalis powder and seal them tightly for long-term storage or immediate use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow capsules helps with neuritis because they contain salicin, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that targets pain and inflammation associated with neuritis. Salicin works by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and swelling in inflamed tissues. Additionally, white willow capsules also have an antioxidant effect, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can exacerbate neuritic symptoms. As a result, white willow capsules provide natural relief from painful and debilitating neuritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Salicin: A natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory compound found in white willow bark, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain associated with neuritis.
Methyl salicylate: Another active compound in white willow bark, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to numb the affected nerves and reduce discomfort.
Fenolic acids: A type of antioxidant present in white willow bark, which helps to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation that can exacerbate neuritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of White Willow contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.
Roots: The roots of White Willow have been traditionally used to treat various inflammatory conditions, including neuritis, due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean salix alba bark by cutting into small pieces and rinsing thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the cleaned bark in a single layer on paper towels or trays for at least 24 hours before grinding.
Grind the dried salix alba bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered bark with 250mg of vegetable cellulose capsules and fill each capsule with 500mg of the herbal mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw capsules helps with neuritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the nerves. The herb's triterpene glycosides and flavonoids work synergistically to soothe irritated nerve endings and promote healing. Additionally, devil's claw has been traditionally used to alleviate joint and muscle pain, which can also be beneficial in managing neuritis symptoms. By targeting the underlying inflammation and discomfort, devil's claw capsules provide natural relief from painful nerve conditions such as neuritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Devil's Claw contains anti-inflammatory compounds like harpagosides and cactus-derived steroids that help reduce inflammation in the nerves, alleviating symptoms of neuritis.

Pain-relieving properties: The active ingredients in Devil's Claw capsules have been shown to have analgesic and antispasmodic effects, helping to relieve pain, stiffness, and numbness associated with neuritis.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Devil's Claw may help protect the nerves from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in Harpagosides, a key compound that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with neuritis.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated nerve tissues, making it an effective component for treating neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried harpagophytum procumbens roots in the morning after dew has evaporated and before midday sun intensity increases.
Cleanly chop the harvested roots into small pieces to release their medicinal properties.
Combine 250 milligrams of chopped root material with 500 milliliters of cold water in a glass jar and let it steep for two hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid extract.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the prepared herbal liquid extract and seal immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mojave yucca capsules helps with neuritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and pain in the nerves. Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in these capsules have potent neuroprotective effects, shielding neurons from damage caused by free radicals and other toxins that contribute to neuritis.
Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids in Mojave Yucca capsules exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce pain, inflammation, and swelling associated with neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Mojave Yucca plant contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.
Rhizomes: Rhizomes are underground stems that store energy-rich sap, containing compounds like steroidal saponins that can help reduce inflammation and ease nerve irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried yucca schidigera root powder from a reputable supplier.
Grind the powder into a fine consistency using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 2500mg of the ground yucca powder with 100mg of gelatin capsules in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Fill 100 capsules with the yucca mixture using a capsule filler machine within 30 seconds.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape capsules helps with neuritis because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and pain in the nerves. The antioxidants, such as resveratrol, scavenge for free radicals that can damage nerve tissue, while the anti-inflammatory compounds, like flavonoids, reduce inflammation and ease discomfort. Flavonoids: Flavonoids like quercetin have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the nerve tissues, providing relief from neuritic pain and discomfort.
Polyphenols: Polyphenolic compounds found in herbal grape capsules may help protect the nerves from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy nerve function and reducing the risk of neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the grape plant have been traditionally used to treat neuritis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce pain and swelling in the nerves.
Seeds: Grape seeds contain a rich source of omega-3 fatty acids, which have been shown to improve nerve function and reduce inflammation, making them effective in treating neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried vitis vinifera leaves from reputable sources weighing approximately 500 grams.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for about 30 seconds.
Sift the powdered leaves through a fine-mesh sieve to ensure uniform particle size measuring about 1-2 mm.
Mix the sifted powder with 250 grams of tapioca starch and 10 grams of silicon dioxide in a large bowl until well-combined.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture using an encapsulation machine or manual process, yielding approximately 200 capsules per batch.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Papaya capsules helps with neuritis because they contain papain, an enzyme that breaks down proteins and reduces inflammation in the body. This natural anti-inflammatory property can help alleviate symptoms of neuritis, such as numbness, tingling, and burning sensations in the hands and feet. Additionally, the antioxidants present in papaya capsules may also help protect nerve cells from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall nerve health and function.
remedy_properties: Papain: Papain is an enzyme found in papaya that helps break down proteins and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.

Vitamin C: High levels of vitamin C in papaya capsules have been shown to improve blood circulation and oxygenation to nerve tissues, reducing numbness and tingling sensations often experienced with neuritis.
Beta-carotene: Beta-carotene is a potent antioxidant that helps protect nerve cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the papaya plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat nerve-related disorders, making them a good choice for manufacturing medicinal capsules for neuritis.
Fruits: Papaya fruits contain enzymes like papain and chymopapain that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried carica papaya leaves from organic farms.
Dry the papaya leaves in a warm oven at 60°c for 6 hours.
Grind 150g of dried papaya leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 3g of ground papaya leaf powder with 97g of gelatin and 20ml of distilled water to form a paste.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared papaya leaf paste and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with neuritis because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the nerves. The extracts of licorice root, particularly glycyrrhizin, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating inflammation and discomfort associated with neuritis. Additionally, licorice capsules may also help to improve nerve function by increasing blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing numbness or tingling sensations.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root contains flavonoids and polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce pain and swelling associated with neuritis.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin, can help protect against oxidative stress and damage to nerves, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Adaptogenic properties: Licorice root is an adaptogen, meaning it helps the body adapt to stress and promotes balance in the body's response to inflammation and injury, which can aid in reducing symptoms of neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of neuritis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of licorice root are used to make medicated oil which is applied topically to relieve pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried licorice root weighing 500 grams and wash thoroughly.
Dry the washed root in a low-temperature oven at 60°c for two hours.
Grind the dried root into fine powder using a spice grinder for ten minutes.
Mix 20% powdered licorice root with 80% acacia powder filling capsules with 500 milligrams each weighing 250 grams.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew capsules helps with neuritis because they contain parthenolide, a sesquiterpene lactone that has potent anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. When taken orally, feverfew capsules can help to reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with neuritis, such as numbness, tingling, or burning sensations in the affected nerves. The active compounds in feverfew may also help to stabilize and repair damaged nerve tissue, promoting healing and reducing the risk of chronic or recurrent episodes of neuritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Feverfew contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling and pain associated with neuritis in the nerves.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in feverfew capsules help protect nerve cells from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress that can contribute to neuritic symptoms.

Cytokine modulation: Feverfew has been shown to modulate the production of cytokines, which are proteins that promote inflammation, helping to reduce the inflammatory response and alleviate neuritis-related pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.
Leaves: Feverfew leaves contain parthenolide, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, providing relief from nerve-related pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried tanacetum parthenium leaves and flowers from trusted sources for quality.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 10:1 ratio of the powdered tanacetum parthenium with 100mg of gelatin capsules or vegetarian capsule shells.
Fill the capsules manually or use an automatic capsule filler for precision accuracy.
Store the filled capsules in airtight containers at room temperature and use within 6 months of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap capsules helps with neuritis because they contain Scutellaria lateriflora, a herb that has been traditionally used to calm irritated nerves and reduce inflammation. The active compounds in skullcap, such as baicalin and wogonoside, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and protect the nervous system. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, skullcap capsules can help to alleviate symptoms of neuritis, including pain, numbness, and tingling, allowing for faster recovery and improved overall nerve function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Skullcap capsules have been shown to reduce inflammation, which can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with neuritis by decreasing swelling and irritation in the affected nerves.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal skullcap capsules contain antioxidants that help protect nerve cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of neuritis by reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall nerve health.

Anxiolytic (stress-reducing) properties: Skullcap capsules have been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, which can exacerbate neuritis symptoms such as tingling, numbness, or burning sensations in the hands and feet; by reducing stress and promoting relaxation, skullcap may help alleviate these symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the skullcap plant contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help relieve neuritis symptoms.
Rhizomes: Rhizomes of the skullcap plant are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in treating neuritis-induced pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried scutellaria lateriflora roots in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency retention.
Measure out one part by weight of dried scutellaria lateriflora root and mix with one half part by weight of carrier powder such as silica or calcium carbonate.
Combine the herbal blend with 10% of its weight in glycerin and 5% of its weight in distilled water to create a paste-like mixture.
Fill empty #0 capsules with the herbal paste mixture, using approximately 500 milligrams per capsule for standard dosing requirements.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight or moisture for up to one year.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to neuritis that are treated with herbal capsules are various forms of neuralgia, sciatica, and peripheral neuropathy. Herbal remedies like turmeric, ginger, and passionflower help alleviate symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and pain in the hands and feet. Other herbs like boswellia and ashwagandha reduce inflammation and improve nerve function, providing relief from conditions that cause neural irritation and discomfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with neuritis is a blend of St. John's Wort, Passionflower, and Ginkgo Biloba. St. John's Wort helps reduce inflammation and pain, while Passionflower calms the nervous system and promotes relaxation. Ginkgo Biloba improves blood flow to the nerves, promoting healing and regeneration. This combination can help alleviate symptoms of neuritis, such as numbness, tingling, and weakness, and promote overall nerve health. Consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal supplements.
